compounddef.basecompoundref: nanoflann::KDTreeBaseClass< KDTreeSingleIndexDynamicAdaptor_< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, -1, uint32_t >, Distance, DatasetAdaptor, -1, uint32_t >

detaileddescription.para: Find set of nearest neighbors to vec[0:dim-1]. Their indices are stored inside the result object. This is the core search function, all others are wrappers around this one.

parameterdescription.para: The result object in which the indices of the nearest-neighbors are stored.
The vector of the query point for which to search the nearest neighbors.

simplesect.para: True if the requested neighbors could be found.
If L2 norms are used, all returned distances are actually squared distances.

memberdef.argsstring: (const ElementType *query_point, const Size num_closest, IndexType *out_indices, DistanceType *out_distances, const SearchParameters &searchParams={}) const
memberdef.name: knnSearch

detaileddescription.para:
Find the "num_closest" nearest neighbors to the query_point[0:dim-1]. Their indices are stored inside the result object.
radiusSearch, findNeighbors
Number N of valid points in the result set.
If L2 norms are used, all returned distances are actually squared distances.
Only the first N entries in out_indices and out_distances will be valid. Return may be less than num_closest only if the number of elements in the tree is less than num_closest.

memberdef.argsstring: (const ElementType *query_point, const DistanceType &radius, std::vector< ResultItem< IndexType, DistanceType >> &IndicesDists, const SearchParameters &searchParams={}) const
memberdef.name: radiusSearch

detaileddescription.para: Find all the neighbors to query_point[0:dim-1] within a maximum radius. The output is given as a vector of pairs, of which the first element is a point index and the second the corresponding distance. Previous contents of IndicesDists are cleared.
If searchParams.sorted==true, the output list is sorted by ascending distances.
For a better performance, it is advisable to do a .reserve() on the vector if you have any wild guess about the number of expected matches.

simplesect.para: The number of points within the given radius (i.e. indices.size() or dists.size() )
If L2 norms are used, search radius and all returned distances are actually squared distances.

detaileddescription.para:
Just like radiusSearch() but with a custom callback class for each point found in the radius of the query. See the source of RadiusResultSet<> as a start point for your own classes.
radiusSearch

memberdef.type: typename nanoflann::KDTreeBaseClass< nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexDynamicAdaptor_< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >, Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >

detaileddescription.para: Define "BoundingBox" as a fixed-size or variable-size container depending on "DIM"

memberdef.definition: using nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexDynamicAdaptor_< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::distance_vector_t = typename Base::distance_vector_t
memberdef.name: distance_vector_t

detaileddescription.para: Define "distance_vector_t" as a fixed-size or variable-size container depending on "DIM"

memberdef.definition: const DatasetAdaptor& nanoflann::KDTreeSingleIndexDynamicAdaptor_< Distance, DatasetAdaptor, DIM, IndexType >::dataset_
memberdef.name: dataset_

detaileddescription.para: The dataset used by this index

memberdef.argsstring: (const Dimension dimensionality, const DatasetAdaptor &inputData, std::vector< int > &treeIndex, const KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptorParams &params=KDTreeSingleIndexAdaptorParams())
memberdef.name: KDTreeSingleIndexDynamicAdaptor_

detaileddescription.para: KDTree constructor
Refer to docs in README.md or online in https://github.com/jlblancoc/nanoflann

The KD-Tree point dimension (the length of each point in the datase, e.g. 3 for 3D points) is determined by means of:
The DIM template parameter if >0 (highest priority)
Otherwise, the dimensionality parameter of this constructor.

parameterdescription.para: Dataset with the input features. Its lifetime must be equal or longer than that of the instance of this class.
Basically, the maximum leaf node size

detaileddescription.para: Explicitly default the copy constructor
Assignment operator definiton
Builds the index

memberdef.argsstring: (RESULTSET &result_set, const ElementType *vec, const NodePtr node, DistanceType mindist, distance_vector_t &dists, const float epsError) const
memberdef.name: searchLevel

detaileddescription.para:
Performs an exact search in the tree starting from a node.
RESULTSET
Should be any ResultSet<DistanceType>

Stores the index in a binary file. IMPORTANT NOTE: The set of data points is NOT stored in the file, so when loading the index object it must be constructed associated to the same source of data points used while building it. See the example: examples/saveload_example.cpp
loadIndex

Loads a previous index from a binary file. IMPORTANT NOTE: The set of data points is NOT stored in the file, so the index object must be constructed associated to the same source of data points used while building the index. See the example: examples/saveload_example.cpp
loadIndex
kd-tree dynamic index
Contains the k-d trees and other information for indexing a set of points for nearest-neighbor matching.
The class "DatasetAdaptor" must provide the following interface (can be non-virtual, inlined methods):

parameterdescription.para: The distance metric to use: nanoflann::metric_L1, nanoflann::metric_L2, nanoflann::metric_L2_Simple, etc.
Dimensionality of data points (e.g. 3 for 3D points)
Type of the arguments with which the data can be accessed (e.g. float, double, int64_t, T*)
